mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary pressures.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising oil prices signal supply chain disruptions, increasing corporate costs and reducing earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by declining unemployment and strong payroll gains, drives wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where both UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and consumer spending indicate strong demand fundamentals, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS rise.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth acceleration above 3% signals robust economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of quarters with GDP growth above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening, raising borrowing costs and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment above 4.5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.
